claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first check the official guidance on eligibility rules.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Do you have any children under the age of 16 (or under 20 if they're in approved education or training)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have two children - Alex who is 8 years old, and Blake who is 20.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To assess eligibility for Alex and Blake, I need to understand a bit more about their circumstances.

Let me start with Blake, who is 20 years old. Is Blake currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, Blake wouldn't be eligible for Child Benefit as they are 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit typically stops at age 16, or can continue until age 20 if the child is in approved education or training.

Now, let me ask about Alex who is 8 years old. Do you live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live with Alex here in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment for Alex.

Are you responsible for Alex's care? For example, do you provide for their day-to-day needs like food, clothes, and accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I take care of all of Alex's day-to-day needs - food, clothes, school things, everything really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just one final question to complete the assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, here's my assessment:

**For Alex (8 years old): You ARE eligible for Child Benefit**

Alex meets all the eligibility criteria:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live with Alex in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex's day-to-day care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**For Blake (20 years old): You are NOT eligible for Child Benefit**

Unfortunately, Blake doesn't meet the age criteria because:
- Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training
- Child Benefit can only continue past age 16 if the child is under 20 AND in approved education or training

**Next steps:**
You can apply for Child Benefit for Alex. If Blake were to start approved education or training in the future and is still under 20, you could potentially claim for them at that time.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), the claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care including food, clothes and school needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK, and they'll usually be responsible if they live with the child. All criteria are met for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit normally only continues past age 16 if the child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 and not in approved education or training, they do not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (8 years old) and Blake (20 years old). The claimant lives with both children in Manchester, UK, and is responsible for their care. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria as they are under 16, live with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming for them. Blake does not meet the eligibility criteria as they are 20 years old and not in approved education or training, which is required for Child Benefit to continue past age 16.

Alex meets all the eligibility criteria as they are under 16, live with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming for them. Blake does not meet the eligibility criteria as they are 20 years old and not in approved education or training, which is required for Child Benefit to continue past age 16.
